Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. U+237C ⍼ Is Azimuth 292 upvotes, 28 comments
  2. Tony Hoare has died 1770 upvotes, 226 comments
  3. Mesh over Bluetooth LE, TCP, or Reticulum 90 upvotes, 10 comments
  4. Julia Snail – An Emacs Development Environment for Julia Like Clojure's Cider 64 upvotes, 7 comments
  5. Invoker Commands API 86 upvotes, 15 comments
  6. Pike: To Exit or Not to Exit 21 upvotes, 2 comments
  7. Writing my own text editor, and daily-driving it 99 upvotes, 25 comments
  8. Building a TB-303 from Scratch 15 upvotes, 1 comments
  9. TADA: Fast, Reliable Speech Generation Through Text-Acoustic Synchronization 31 upvotes, 5 comments
  10. Standardizing source maps 27 upvotes, 4 comments
  11. Universal vaccine against respiratory infections and allergens 245 upvotes, 79 comments
  12. Zig – Type Resolution Redesign and Language Changes 205 upvotes, 66 comments
  13. FFmpeg-over-IP – Connect to remote FFmpeg servers 182 upvotes, 59 comments
  14. AutoKernel: Autoresearch for GPU Kernels 17 upvotes, 2 comments
  15. Cloudflare crawl endpoint 292 upvotes, 115 comments
  16. SSH Secret Menu 198 upvotes, 75 comments
  17. Create value for others and don’t worry about the returns 300 upvotes, 140 comments
  18. EQT eyes potential $6B sale of Linux pioneer SUSE, sources say 51 upvotes, 18 comments
  19. Levels of Agentic Engineering 174 upvotes, 86 comments
  20. Surpassing vLLM with a Generated Inference Stack 36 upvotes, 14 comments
  21. Meta acquires Moltbook 493 upvotes, 327 comments
  22. Launch HN: RunAnywhere (YC W26) – Faster AI Inference on Apple Silicon 210 upvotes, 130 comments
  23. Debian decides not to decide on AI-generated contributions 324 upvotes, 252 comments
  24. Yann LeCun raises $1B to build AI that understands the physical world 459 upvotes, 376 comments
  25. RISC-V Is Sloooow 237 upvotes, 226 comments
  26. Launch HN: Didit (YC W26) – Stripe for Identity Verification 68 upvotes, 59 comments
  27. Agents that run while I sleep 326 upvotes, 344 comments
  28. Roblox is minting teen millionaires 135 upvotes, 143 comments
  29. I'm going to build my own OpenClaw, with blackjack and bun 27 upvotes, 21 comments
  30. Support for Aquantia AQC113 and AQC113C Ethernet Controllers on FreeBSD 7 upvotes, 3 comments

Last generated 5:30pm, 11 March 2026. Source here.
